description_long: This object is a shotgun with a robust, elongated body and a symmetrical, streamlined design. The barrel appears to be a single, solid tube extending from the main body, with no visible ribbing or venting along its length. The stock is integrated into the rear, forming a continuous line with the receiver, and features a full, traditional shape for shoulder support. The fore-end is slightly thicker, suggesting a pump-action mechanism, and a trigger guard is visible beneath the receiver, shaped in a rounded, protective loop. The overall finish is dark, likely matte or semi-gloss, with minimal visible ornamentation or color variation. The object is constructed primarily from metal, with possible polymer or wood components in the stock and fore-end.
description: A dark, streamlined shotgun with a single barrel, full stock, and a rounded trigger guard, featuring a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the single barrel and continuous stock.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow barrel opening and the slim profile of the shotgun.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the features of view 0 with the same barrel and stock details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the stock and the alignment of the barrel with the receiver.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth, continuous loop and no visible finger grooves
